name: task-grooming-catches-sibling-epic-overlap
tags: task-grooming, tasks__neighbors, sibling epic, overlap, duplicate work, pre-implementation, task:380140fa
body:
Running /task-grooming (which calls tasks__neighbors on the task, not just re-reading its own body) surfaced a same-week sibling epic covering overlapping territory that wasn't visible from the task/epic body alone — the two epics had been created independently, one day apart, by different lines of conversation, without either referencing the other.

Why: task:2f0fb672 (Execution Philosophy) and task:f66cccbe (Task-guidance features — keep an active task on track) both target "steer an active task's behavior" from different angles (standing injected contract vs. reactive nudges) and were about to independently reinvent the same reflection/nudge mechanism.

How to apply: always run tasks__neighbors (or /task-grooming, which calls it) before starting implementation on a freshly created epic/task, even one that feels novel — semantic neighbor search surfaces overlap that keyword search or memory of the current conversation misses. When overlap is found, link the tasks via tasks__link_tasks(relation_type="relates_to") rather than just noting it in the body, so the relationship survives independent of either task's text.
